title: tk graph: report needs-planning for childless epics instead of an empty graph
description:
Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §1 'tk changes'. Consumes query.EpicsNeedingPlanning (already in internal/query/plan.go when this tick runs).

File: cmd/tk/cmd/graph.go.

Behavior: when the target epic has zero children (any status):
- Human output: instead of an empty wave listing, print a short notice: 'Epic <id> has no child ticks — it needs planning. Flesh it out into ticks (see the ticks skill roadmap guidance), then re-run tk graph <id>.' Use query.EpicsNeedingPlanning to distinguish 'needs planning' (unblocked) from 'blocked — will need planning when <blockers> close' and say which.
- JSON output (additive): add NeedsPlanning bool `json:"needs_planning"` to the top-level graphOutput struct. true when the epic has zero children; existing fields keep their current shapes (waves empty, stats zeroed) so existing consumers are unaffected.

Do NOT change behavior for epics that have children. Do not modify next.go or internal/query (other ticks own those files).

Tests: graph.go's output structs are package-local — add a focused test (cmd/tk/cmd/graph_test.go if none exists) for the zero-children JSON path if the command logic is testable without a full repo; otherwise verify end-to-end like the sibling tick: build tk, /tmp fixture repo with a childless epic, assert needs_planning:true in tk graph <id> --json, and document the commands in your report.

Run: go test ./... && go build ./...
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... passes; tk graph <childless-epic> --json contains needs_planning:true and human output explains planning is needed (fixture-verified)
